{{Quote|The Mandalorian Wars were a series of massacres that masked another war, a war of conversion. Culminating in a final atrocity that no Jedi could walk away from… save one.|Kreia|<ref name="KOTOR II" />}}
The '''Mandalorian Wars''' was the term given to the sixteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of conflict between the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior culture and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] that began in [[3976 BBY]] with the [[Battle of Althir (first)|Battle of Althir]]. Occurring two [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] after the end of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the Mandalorian Wars spanned almost two decades themselves, though [[Historian/Legends|historians]] often disagreed on the endpoints of the conflict; the Mandalorians raided [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] for over a decade before they actually came into conflict with the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]] in [[3965 BBY]]. Led by [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], who pioneered the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Neo-Crusader]] movement with the help of his [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], [[Cassus Fett]], the Mandalorians conquered systems along the eastern edge of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Though their conquests included the [[Battle of Cathar|near-extinction]] of the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] [[Species/Legends|species]], it was not until their assaults on [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] near the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] that they drew the Republic's attention. After a year of small conflicts known as the False War, the Mandalorians broke through the Republic's lines and [[Taris Siege|besieged]] Taris in [[3963 BBY]], and then invaded the Republic through three separate corridors in what became known as the Onslaught.

The tide ultimately turned when a group of interventionist [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] known as the [[Revanchist]]s, led by the charismatic [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and his friend [[Darth Malak|Malak]], joined the Republic Military in combating the Mandalorians. A tactical genius, Revan won several victories against the Mandalorians and began to reclaim lost territory, prompting [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Tol Cressa]] to appoint him [[Supreme Commander (Republic)|Supreme Commander]] in [[3962 BBY]]. Driving back the Mandalorians, Revan forced a final showdown with Mandalore the Ultimate at the [[Battle of Malachor V]] in [[3960 BBY]]. Revan personally defeated Mandalore in single combat as the Republic and Mandalorian [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] battled above [[Malachor V]], and the activation of the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] known as the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]] devastated both the planet and the participating fleets.

The Mandalorian Wars had long-reaching consequences: Revan forced the defeated Mandalorians to disarm and hid [[Mandalore's Mask]], the symbol of their leadership, and both he and Malak were [[Mission to Dromund Kaas|turned]] to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] while investigating the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Unidentified Sith emissary|influence]] that had caused Mandalore to go to war. Many of the [[Republic soldier|soldiers]] and Jedi who followed Revan in the Mandalorian Wars joined the two new [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords of the Sith]] as they formed their [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|own Sith Empire]] and invaded the Republic in a subsequent conflict known as the [[Jedi Civil War]].

==Prelude==
===Reconstruction===
{{Quote|Exar Kun was defeated, but the war left both the Republic and our own Order severely weakened. For twenty years, we struggled to rebuild, trying to erase the scars of the terrible conflict.|Dorak|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
[[File:Mandalore_the_Indomitable.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Mandalore the Ultimate (left) and his predecessor, Mandalore the Indomitable (right)]]
The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] Wars were named for the Mandalorians, a warrior culture that had clashed frequently with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in the past. The Mandalorian Wars occurred two [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] after the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], a conflict fought between the Republic and the forces of two former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]: [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]]. Both had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and declared themselves to be [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], and Qel-Droma earned the Mandalorians' loyalty after defeating their leader, [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], in a [[Duel on Kuar|duel]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kuar]]. [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore's]] forces served Qel-Droma ably in the Sith War, but in the last [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the conflict, the Mandalorians' attempt to [[Battle of Onderon (Great Sith War)|conquer]] the planet [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] took a disastrous turn with the arrival of the Republic. Mandalore ordered his [[Mandalorian Crusaders]] to retreat to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]] to lose their pursuers in the moon's fierce jungles, but the warrior's [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droid]] was shot down, and he was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by predatory animals. [[Mandalore's Mask]], the symbol of leadership among the Mandalorians, was discovered by a [[Taung/Legends|Taung]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] who succeeded the Indomitable as Mandalore. Most believed that the soldier assumed the name [[Mandalore the Ultimate]],<ref name="Warfare" /> but a few believed that Mandalore the Ultimate usurped the title from the [[Unidentified Mandalore|true Mandalore]] who had found the Mask. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] after the Mandalorian Wars, that supposed "true Mandalore"—a Taung like the Ultimate—claimed that Mandalore the Ultimate had stolen the title and mask from him.<ref name="Campaign" />

In the decades that followed the Sith War, the Republic and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] experienced an era of peace and rebuilding known as the [[Restoration Period]]. To restore the Republic's economy and infrastructure, a group of [[Politics/Legends|politicians]] decided to guarantee various corporations throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] safe passage and trade along the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] in exchange for commercial investment in the Republic. As a result, the Republic was able to rebuild its [[Republic Military/Legends|military]] and economy, supplying much-needed goods to the rest of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], and [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Scout/Legends|explorers]] resumed exploration of the galaxy to discover new routes. However, while the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] was bolstered by the influx of commerce and funding, taking the place of corporate defense forces, some groups attempted to manipulate the government for their own profit.<ref name="Campaign" /> The [[City/Legends|city]]-world of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], an influential trade world in the [[Northern Dependencies/Legends|Northern Dependencies]] region, was admitted to the Republic in [[3966 BBY/Legends|3966 BBY]]<ref name="Warfare" /> after a number of corporations, including [[Lhosan Industries]], bribed Republic [[Senator/Legends|senators]] into admitting the planet.<ref name="Handbook">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]''</ref>

The Mandalorians also continued their nomadic existence under Mandalore the Ultimate's leadership, rebuilding their ranks.<ref name="Campaign" /> Less than a decade after the end of the Sith War, Mandalore the Ultimate was approached by the [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Demagol|Antos Wyrick]], who had been Mandalore the Indomitable's own student during the Sith War. Wyrick had acquired a [[Gene/Legends|genetic]] sample from the [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]], Ulic Qel-Droma's teacher, and believed that he could [[Bioengineering/Legends|genetically engineer]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] who could be used as a kind of Mandalorian Jedi. Mandalore agreed to Wyrick's proposal, and the Zeltron started the [[New Generation Project]], which was headquartered on the planet [[Osadia]] and sought to produce [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-sensitive children from [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] [[Parent/Legends|parents]]. However, Wyrick's dealings with [[the Crucible]] [[Slavery/Legends|enslaving]] organization led to the project's destruction, as the Crucible raided the [[New Generation Academy|Osadia School]]<ref name="KOTOR 48">[[Knights of the Old Republic 48|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 48]]</ref> around a decade after [[3988 BBY]]<ref name="KOTOR 46">[[Knights of the Old Republic 46|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 46]]</ref> and kidnapped all of Wyrick's students. Wyrick was unable to recover his students after years of searching and ultimately rejoined the Mandalorian cause as Mandalore's chief scientist, [[Torture/Legends|torturing]] and experimenting on captured Jedi in order to discover the source of their [[Force power/Legends|powers]]. Wyrick became known as "Demagol," a contraction of the [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']] term ''demar agol'' and a name that literally meant "the flesh carver." Another of Mandalore's [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenants]] was [[Cassus Fett]], a warrior who became Mandalore's aide-de-camp<ref name="Campaign" /> and top strategist.<ref name="NEC" />

===Influenced to war===
{{Quote|The Sith took the remains to give to his Master, and in exchange he told Mandalore of a vision his Master had of the Mandalorians rising up against the Republic. He told him they would conquer world after world, crushing their enemies until the Republic collapsed in on itself. He promised the Mandalorians a glorious victory, and Mandalore believed him.|Revan|<ref name="Revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Mandalore_The_Ultimate_by_Darren_Tan.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Mandalore the Ultimate inspires his new followers.]]
In [[3978 BBY]], Mandalore was approached by a [[Unidentified Sith emissary|member]] of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Species/Legends|species]] who introduced himself as an emissary of a powerful [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], and the emissary enlisted the warrior's help in locating the [[Dramath the Second's tomb|tomb]] of his master's rival, [[Dramath the Second]], on the planet [[Rekkiad]]. Upon discovering the [[Burial/Legends|tomb]], the emissary told Mandalore of a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] that his master had experienced of the Mandalorians sweeping across the Republic and conquering many worlds before ultimately crushing the Republic in a glorious victory. Unbeknownst to Mandalore, the emissary was a servant of the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] of a [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|hidden Empire]], and the warrior was being [[Qâzoi Kyantuska|manipulated]] through the Force by the emissary—the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] sought to use the Mandalorians as a weapon against the Republic and thereby test the Republic's defenses. Completely under the Sith's spell, Mandalore took up the idea of invading the Republic as his own and began to reorganize and marshal the Mandalorian clans in preparation for the forthcoming battles.<ref name="Revan" /> Mandalore the Ultimate returned to [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalorian Space]] and instituted a number of reforms: Firstly, the Mandalorians would begin to accept non-Taung warriors who proved themselves in battle and upheld the Mandalorian warrior code. Secondly, the Mandalorians would no longer pillage worlds and then move on to the next target; instead, they would hold their territory and build an industrial society based on the Mandalorian code.<ref name="Warfare" />

Mandalore's new vision of society was one where warriors would rule and be supported by [[Farm/Legends|farmers]], [[Artisan/Legends|artisans]], and manufacturers who had accepted the Mandalorian way. As a result of his decision, the ranks of the Mandalorians rapidly swelled with all manner of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and [[Alien/Legends|aliens]], primarily Mandalorian vassals, such as [[Mandallian Giant]]s and [[Jakelian]]s. This new system was dubbed the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Neo-Crusader]] movement,<ref name="Warfare" /> and with Cassus Fett's aid,<ref name="KOTOR 20">[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]]</ref> Mandalore the Ultimate pioneered the movement as a successor to the Indomitable's Crusaders. The Neo-Crusader name originated as an extremist cult that called for the reestablishment of the Crusader traditions, and Mandalore adapted their views to suit his own ends—the Neo-Crusader movement, though slow to take hold, gradually shaped the Mandalorians into a more disciplined and organized fighting force.<ref name="Campaign" /> Fett spearheaded the movement in Mandalore's name while also serving his commander as a strategist and [[Aide/Legends|aide]],<ref name="KOTOR 20" /> and the Mandalorians began constructing new [[Warship/Legends|warships]] at [[Breshig]] and [[Arda II|Arda]]; these [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were built using war matériel stolen from the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] at [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]] and [[Abhean/Legends|Abhean]] during the Sith War. As the Neo-Crusaders expanded their domain, they inducted members from varying species, such as [[Hrakian]]s, [[Togruta/Legends|Togruta]], [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronians]], [[Nalroni/Legends|Nalroni]], [[Zygerrian/Legends|Zygerrians]], [[Drackmarian]]s, and [[Elomin/Legends|Elomin]].<ref name="Warfare" />

==The war==
====War in the Outer Rim====
{{Quote|We started by conquering worlds just outside the Republic. We did it quietly so the Republic wouldn't really know what was going on until too late. When we finally did hit the Republic worlds, they had no idea we were coming.|Canderous Ordo|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
[[File:Battle_of_Cathar.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Battle of Cathar]]
In [[3976 BBY]], Mandalore the Ultimate's forces made their first move: a [[Battle of Althir (first)|raid]] on the industrialized planet [[Althir III]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. A [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of Neo-Crusader warships stormed the world,<ref name="Warfare" /> though the battle lasted a total of five days, as the [[Althiri]] managed to hold off the Mandalorians—a stalemate that was finally broken by the unit of a young warrior named [[Canderous Ordo]], who was tasked with feinting against one of the enemy flanks in order to draw the enemy out of formation. However, Ordo saw an opening in the enemy ranks and took the initiative, forcing the Althiri to fend off assaults on two sides and exposing their vulnerable [[Command ship/Legends|command ships]]. Ordo's forces quickly eradicated the Althiri fleet, which was ten times the size of their own, and Ordo was rewarded with command over an entire sub-sect of his [[Clan Ordo|clan]].<ref name="KOTOR" /> Althiri became a productive part of Mandalore's war machine, and [[3973 BBY|three years later]], Mandalore's forces struck at another world—[[Cathar (location)/Legends|Cathar]], home of the [[Feline/Legends|feline]] [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] species.<ref name="Warfare" />

The [[Battle of Cathar]]<ref name="Campaign" /> was led by Cassus Fett, who directed his forces to herd the defenseless Cathar population into the [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]]. There, Fett's forces massacred the Cathar, despite the protests of a [[Unidentified Mandalorian female|single Mandalorian woman]], who was killed along with the Cathar.<ref name="KOTOR 42" /> Basilisk [[Battle droid/Legends|war droids]] swept across the planet, killing the remaining population—only a few hundred survived,<ref name="TORE">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> with over 90 percent of the Cathar population dying during the massacre.<ref name="Atlas" /> The Republic learned of the event but chose to suppress news of the battle in order to prevent rioting and panic,<ref name="Campaign" /> and it mobilized the Republic Navy to guard Dxun—still home to some defiant Mandalorian clans—and the planet Taris,<ref name="Warfare" /> which sat along the trade route known as the [[Mandalorian Road]] and linked the planets [[Corsin/Legends|Corsin]] and [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]].<ref name="Atlas" />

Over the next eight years, the Mandalorians also [[Raid on Corsin|raided]] [[Raid on Azure|worlds]] such as Corsin and [[Azure/Legends|Azure]], and Mandalorian warships expanded their territory into the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]] and [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] on the eastern edge of the galaxy. Starting with the [[First battle of the Lianna system|capture]] of the [[Lianna system/Legends|Lianna system]], the Mandalorians traveled up the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] and [[Battle of the Makem system (Mandalorian Wars)|took]] [[Battle of the Quermia system|the]] [[Makem system|Makem]] and [[Quermia system/Legends|Quermia systems]] before heading southeast along the [[Overic Griplink/Legends|Overic Griplink]] hyperroute, [[Battle of the Kaelta system|conquering]] the [[Kaelta system]] and then [[Battle of the Florn system|taking]] the [[Florn system/Legends|Florn system]]. At the same [[Time/Legends|time]], another campaign was launched from the Lianna [[Star system/Legends|system]]—the Mandalorians first [[Battle of the Jaminere system|took]] the [[Jaminere system/Legends|Jaminere system]], then [[Battle of the Sy Myrth system|headed south]] to the [[Sy Myrth system/Legends|Sy Myrth system]], and finally intruded on [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] Space by [[Battle of the Taskeed system|taking]] the [[Taskeed system/Legends|Taskeed system]], [[Battle of the Dennogra system|capturing]] the [[Dennogra system/Legends|Dennogra system]], and then [[Battle of the Dernatine system (Mandalorian Wars)|seizing]] the [[Dernatine system]]. The Hutts resorted to hiring [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Army/Legends|armies]] to defend their territories, but frequently the Mandalorians simply recruited the mercenaries into their own ranks.<ref name="Atlas" /> Some Neo-Crusaders suggested using [[kryotin]], a powder from the planet [[Veroleem]], as a [[Bioweapon/Legends|bioweapon]] to soften up targets, but most Mandalorians were disgusted by the idea.<ref name="Unknown">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref> A forward supply post named [[Unity (outpost)|Unity]] was established on the planet [[Caillte]] to support the planned advance into the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="Intrigue">''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]''</ref>

[[File:RohlanFled.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Rohlan Dyre flees a raid in the early years of the war.]]
[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Rohlan Dyre]] served in the early battles in the Outer Rim but began to question Mandalore's goals and reasoning for testing the Republic's defenses. When his questions went unanswered, Dyre responded by refusing to fight the next time he was called upon. Dyre was placed on the front lines anyway, so he escaped during the fighting and went in search of answers on his own,<ref name="KOTOR 8" /> investigating "[[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] Demagol's" history and talking to people who knew the scientist before he joined the ''Mando'ade''.<ref name="KOTOR 48" /> "Rohlan the Questioner," as he became known, was eventually captured and placed back on the front lines, but he ran away again—a cycle that continued to repeat over the next few years. By [[3964 BBY]], Dyre had been caught and sent back to the front lines six times.<ref name="KOTOR 8" /> In 3966 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], when Taris's resource worlds of [[Jebble]], [[Vanquo]], [[Tarnith]], and [[Suurja]] began to be threatened by the Mandalorians,<ref name="Siege Edition">{{KOTORbackups|15}}</ref> the Republic established the [[Jebble-Vanquo-Tarnith line]], a protective cordon around Taris that linked those three planets; [[Admiral/Legends|Admirals]] [[Jimas Veltraa]] and [[Noma Sommos]] were among the [[Republic officer/Legends|officers]] tasked with holding the line.<ref name="Memorial" />

===The coming storm===
====The False War====
{{Quote|Mandalore spends years picking off unaligned rimworlds—the Senate sits. Mandalore gets too close to one full of Republic business interests—and the Senate throws it Republic membership and a security cordon so long it would take five fleets our size to crew it. I'm sure Mandalore did a little dance when he heard the Republic had pledged to defend Taris. They've called these last few months "War." I think you can see it's nothing of the sort!|Saul Karath|<ref name="KOTOR 8" />}}
[[File:Captured_Jedi.jpg|thumb|left|260px|The Revanchists are captured on Suurja.]]
The Mandalorians' progress came to a halt at the end of the year [[3965 BBY]]—the Republic Navy's forces around Taris blocked the invaders' attempts to capture more [[Star/Legends|star]] systems in the Outer Rim, and the Mandalorians went eight [[Standard month/Legends|months]] without a victory. Finally, the Mandalorians made progress along the Mandalorian Road and [[Battle of Flashpoint Station|captured]] the planet [[Flashpoint (planet)|Flashpoint]], home to the [[Flashpoint Stellar Research Station]], after a brief skirmish with Republic forces.<ref name="Prime Edition">{{KOTORbackups|0}}</ref> Flashpoint Station was repurposed as a [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratory]] for Demagol, a place where captured Jedi were to be brought so that the scientist could experiment on them.<ref name="KOTOR 8" /> A few months later, the Mandalorians made an attempt to [[First battle of Suurja|take]] the [[Agriworld/Legends|agrarian]] world of Suurja,<ref name="Prime Edition" /> which sat along the cordon between Tarnith and Jebble,<ref name="Atlas" /> but they were engaged by Republic forces under the command of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Saul Karath]], and the battle was inconclusive. Over the next six [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]], [[Second battle of Suurja|three]] [[Third battle of Suurja|further]] [[Fourth battle of Suurja|battles]] were fought at Suurja, but all three of the skirmishes were likewise inconclusive.<ref name="Prime Edition" />

Around that time, a charismatic [[Revan/Legends|young Jedi]] began to move through the Jedi Order, calling for Jedi intervention in the conflict with the Mandalorians. He advocated revanchism, or the retaking of territories lost to the Mandalorians, and his views were met with suspicion and hostility by the increasingly conservative [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], which remained wary of intervention due to the threat of the dark side. Encouraged by his Jedi Master, [[Kreia]], the young Jedi began to openly challenge the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Council]] and gathered a following of like-minded Jedi, including his friend [[Darth Malak|Alek]].<ref name="Campaign" /> The movement became known as the [[Revanchist]]s, and their leader was branded "the Jedi's own crusader" by the Republic [[Media/Legends|media]].<ref name="Handbook" /> Defying the Jedi Council's wishes, the "crusader" led Alek and the other Revanchists on a [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] mission to the battlefront, and they stopped on Taris in hopes of recruiting some of the Jedi who were stationed at the planet's [[Jedi Tower]]. When the Jedi failed to sway any of the five Jedi Masters there or their [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]],<ref name="KOTOR 0">[[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]]</ref> they departed for the planet Suurja. However, the "crusader" left his followers on Suurja shortly afterward to investigate Mandalorian activity on Onderon and Dxun, and the Revanchists were unprepared for an [[Ambush on Suurja|ambush]] by Mandalorians led by Rohlan Dyre.<ref name="KOTOR 6">[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]]</ref> Alek and the other Revanchists were taken to Flashpoint, where Demagol tortured and experimented on them in hopes of determining the source of their powers.<ref name="KOTOR 8" />

However, everything changed when the galaxy learned of the [[Padawan Massacre]]. Four of the five Padawans at the Jedi Tower were slain by their Masters, who were members of a secret [[Jedi Covenant]] dedicated to preventing the return of the Sith, and Master [[Lucien Draay]]'s Padawan, [[Zayne Carrick]], was blamed for the murders. Carrick and the [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] [[Con artist/Legends|con man]] [[Marn Hierogryph]] evaded the Covenant Jedi and Taris's [[Police officer/Legends|police officers]], and their inability to capture Carrick sparked a public loss of faith in the Jedi Order's ability to police them. The same day as the Revanchists' capture on Suurja, Carrick was captured by a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Valius Ying]] and brought to the Jedi Tower. The news of Carrick's capture caused planet-wide celebrations on Taris, as companies such as Lhosan Industries had begun to pull out from the planet in light of the Massacre, but when Carrick escaped from the Jedi Tower with the help of his friends aboard the junk hauler [[The Last Resort (starship)|''The Last Resort'']], mass panic and rioting broke out over the following days. The [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police force]] was overwhelmed, with [[Sector Constable|Constable]] [[Noana Sowrs]]'s children being kidnapped in the chaos, and the five Jedi Masters were recalled to the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="KOTOR 6" />

====The Onslaught====
{{Quote|The prologue is over. We're breaking out on the Outer Rim—and more. The real Mandalorian Wars have begun.|Rohlan Dyre|<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 7">[[Knights of the Old Republic 7|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 7]]</ref>}}
[[File:Battle_of_Vanquo.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Republic and Mandalorian fleets clash at the Battle of Vanquo.]]
Seeing the Jedi's withdrawal as an opportunity to take Taris and begin the invasion of the Republic in force, Mandalore the Ultimate ordered his forces to attack the planet Vanquo, thereby ending the eight-month stalemate. At the [[Battle of Vanquo]], Mandalorian forces overwhelmed the Republic Navy's forces and broke the Jebble-Vanquo-Tarnith line, conquering the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] world; at the same time, Mandalorian forces broke through the Republic cordon and headed toward Taris.<ref name="KOTOR 8" /> As the year [[3963 BBY]] began, Admiral Veltraa withdrew his forces closer toward Taris and reformed the remaining fleets under his command into a new defensive line. Admiral Sommos was gravely injured during the fighting, and she was evacuated to the planet [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]]; however, Veltraa was killed when his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Reliance (Mandalorian Wars)|''Reliance'']], was destroyed, and the Mandalorians [[Taris Siege|besieged]] Taris itself.<ref name="Memorial" /> Mandalorian [[Kandosii-type dreadnaught|dreadnaughts]] began to [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombard]] the planet from [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], beginning weeks of devastation as the Mandalorians began to invade the planet, though they were opposed by Taris's meager planetary defense forces, the [[Taris Home Guard]].<ref name="Siege Edition" /> A [[Taris Resistance]] soon formed from the remnants of the planet's police force, and [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gangs]] such as the [[Hidden Beks]] fought the Mandalorian invaders as well.<ref name="KOTOR 22">[[Knights of the Old Republic 22|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 22]]</ref>

However, the Mandalorians also suffered losses on unexpected fronts—the Jedi Council ordered the "crusader" to rescue the captured Jedi at Flashpoint Station, but the Jedi were instead rescued by Zayne Carrick and his allies when Carrick's friend [[Jarael]] was herself taken. In order to rescue Jarael and the other Jedi, Carrick's group tricked the Mandalorians there into believing that the Republic was booby-trapping their ships with explosives and convinced them to flee the station. Demagol was also apparently captured,<ref name="KOTOR 10">[[Knights of the Old Republic 10|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 10]]</ref> but unbeknownst to Carrick and the others, the scientist switched [[Armor/Legends|armors]] with Rohlan Dyre—who had fled the Vanquo battlefront and joined forces with Carrick—and placed Dyre in a coma.<ref name="KOTOR 48" /> Alek and the Revanchists took "Demagol" back to Coruscant, while "Rohlan Dyre" slipped aboard ''The Last Resort''.<ref name="KOTOR 10" />

[[File:Onslaught.png|thumb|left|250px|The path of the Onslaught]]
Not long afterward, the "crusader" reunited with his followers and continued his campaign for Jedi intervention but soon found himself to be a celebrity in the eyes of the Republic. Republic media cast "the Revanchist Leader" as a crusading savior who was wrongfully ignored by the Council, and he soon became known simply as the Revanchist.<ref name="Handbook" /> The Revanchist continued to travel the galaxy and bring his message to all who would listen, and one of the Revanchist Jedi—a Cathar named [[Ferroh]]—brought to his attention the absence of the Cathar species from their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. The Revanchists began investigating the planet, excavating and searching for evidence as to what had happened to the planet's population.<ref name="KOTOR 42" /> As they besieged Taris, the Mandalorians also attacked the Republic through invasion corridors<ref name="Memorial" /> that ran through three adjacent [[Sector/Legends|sectors]]<ref name="KOTOR" />—an offensive that became known as "the Onslaught."<ref name="Memorial" /> The Mandalorians headed west along the Outer Rim and [[Battle of the Zongorlu system|took]] the [[Zongorlu system/Legends|Zongorlu system]] before launching several offensives against planets along the Republic's northern rim:<ref name="Atlas" /> [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] [[Battle of Ord Mantell (Mandalorian Wars)|fell]] to the Mandalorians as they also [[Attacks on the Zabrak colonies|attacked]] a number of colonies of the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] species in the region, and they also [[Battle of Ithor (Mandalorian Wars)|attacked]] [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]], though a Republic counterattack with the support of Zabrak military units repelled the invaders.<ref name="Warfare" />

The Mandalorians then [[First battle of Iridonia (Mandalorian Wars)|attacked]] the Zabrak homeworld of [[Iridonia/Legends|Iridonia]], which they conquered and occupied for quite some time, recruiting some of the natives into their ranks.<ref name="Darkness">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/iridonian|text=Iridonian Darkness|int=Iridonian Darkness}}</ref> Republic and Zabrak forces ultimately [[Second battle of Iridonia (Mandalorian Wars)|liberated]] the planet, but a Mandalorian presence remained in Iridonian society.<ref name="Warfare" /> A second invasion corridor saw the Mandalorians [[Battle of Wayland (Mandalorian Wars)|assault]] Wayland,<ref name="Atlas" /> bombing the surface and wiping out colonists and factories.<ref name="KOTOR 23">[[Knights of the Old Republic 23|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 23]]</ref> The third invasion corridor was aimed [[Core Worlds/Legends|Coreward]]—the Mandalorians [[Capture of Jebble|captured]] Jebble,<ref name="Atlas" /> building a [[Ice Citadel|war forge]] on the [[Water/Legends|ice]] planet that supplied the Mandalorian offensive and also served as a staging point for a planned attack on the Core World of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]].<ref name="KOTOR 25">[[Knights of the Old Republic 25|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 25]]</ref>

===The Mandalorian Invasion===
====The devastation of Serroco====
{{Quote|This… is a new phase of war. We're going to have change the way we look at things.|Saul Karath, after the Mandalorian nuclear attack on Serroco|<ref name="KOTOR 15" />}}
[[File:Nuclear_explosions.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The bombing of Serroco]]
In light of the Onslaught, the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] mobilized the entire Republic Navy to counter the Mandalorian threat.<ref name="KOTOR" /> Around the same time, Mandalorian forces [[First battle of Onderon (Mandalorian Wars)|struck]] at Onderon, besieging the walled city of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]] with basilisk war [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Onderon and its moon Dxun remained under extended Mandalorian control, serving as a staging point later in the conflict.<ref name="Atlas" /> The remains of the Outer Rim fleets fell back to the Core Worlds, where Captain Karath was promoted to [[Rear Admiral/Legends|Rear Admiral]]<ref name="Memorial" /> and a large battle group, designated [[Battle Group Serroco]], was gathered at the planet [[Ralltiir/Legends|Ralltiir]]. Given the [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship]] [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']], Karath was placed in command of the battle group and tasked with the defense of the planet [[Serroco]], which was the next world in the path of the Mandalorians' advance from Jebble. Karath's forces set up camps right next to the stone cities of the native [[Stereb]], and the fleet formed up in a defensive formation around the planet. However, Mandalore the Ultimate viewed this tactic as a defense without honor, as it assumed that the Mandalorians would not attack the civilian cities, so he decided to teach the Republic a lesson.<ref name="KOTOR 14">[[Knights of the Old Republic 14|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 14]]</ref>

Zayne Carrick was on Serroco with Marn Hierogryph while staffing the [[Quartermaster-class supply carrier|''Quartermaster''-class supply carrier]] ''[[Little Bivoli]]'', which was serving the Republic forces as a [[Fringer/Legends|fringer]] provisioning ship on Serroco; the day before Mandalore's attack, Carrick experienced a Force vision of the forthcoming assault.<ref name="KOTOR 14" /> Carrick became determined to warn Karath and slipped aboard Lieutenant [[Carth Onasi]]'s ship, the ''[[Deadweight]]'', to reach the ''Courageous'', but Karath refused to believe him: Karath had seen Carrick's ship, ''The Last Resort'', fleeing the Battle of Vanquo for Mandalorian Space and believed him to be a Mandalorian [[Spy/Legends|spy]]. Ignoring Carrick's explanation that he had been heading for Flashpoint, Karath ordered Carrick's arrest, though he allowed Onasi to try and contact Alek, whom Carrick knew only as "Squint." However, the Mandalorian fleet arrived at the edge of the [[Serroco system/Legends|Serroco system]] only minutes after Onasi left the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], and Karath ordered his ships to raise their [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] when the Mandalorians launched [[Missile/Legends|missiles]].<ref name="KOTOR 15" />

To Karath's shock, the [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear warheads]] swerved around the Republic ships to [[Battle of Serroco|slam]] into Serroco's surface, impacting near twenty-seven population centers and wiping away most of the Stereb cities. The devastation caught most of the Republic ships on the surface by surprise, destroying all but eight of them, and Jedi all across the galaxy felt the simultaneous deaths of thousands through the Force. Fortunately for the Stereb, Onasi had been able to call down emergencies to seventeen of the Stereb cities,<ref name="KOTOR 15" /> leading the inhabitants of those settlements to take shelter in the underground catacombs of the planet and thereby survive the nuclear devastation.<ref name="Handbook" /> The Revanchist Jedi [[Meetra Surik]],<ref name="surik">In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', the main character, [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Meetra Surik]], is mentioned as having been on [[Serroco]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Battle of Serroco|attack]]. Since Jedi involvement in the war had not yet begun, this was rectified by the later ''[[Galactic Republic Defense Ministry Daily Brief KD0092]]'', which states that there were Jedi on regular assignment to Serroco and one was able to get away on one of the few [[Starship/Legends|ships]] to escape. This article makes the natural conclusion that this Jedi was Surik.</ref> who was on assignment at Serroco, managed to escape aboard one of the three military vessels that jumped out of the system before the devastation.<ref name="Defense">{{KOTORbackups|24}}</ref>

The Battle of Serroco saw Karath's forces begin a slow retreat toward the Core as they came under fire from the Mandalorians, and the [[Admiral of the Fleet/Legends|Admiral of the Fleet]] announced his intention to lodge an official protest with the Republic over the military's positioning of its forces near the Stereb cities. Karath sought to retreat to [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] or rendezvous with the [[Tremendous group|''Tremendous'' group]] near the [[Ryyk Nebula/Legends|Ryyk Nebula]] but was ultimately forced to fall back toward the banking planet of [[Telerath/Legends|Telerath]].<ref name="Serroco" /> The ''Courageous'' was soon boarded by [[Neo-Crusader shock trooper]]s, whose assault drove Karath, Onasi, and Captain [[Dallan Morvis]] to take up defensive positions in the ship's brig. They managed to escape the ship with the help of Carrick, who had created an escape route to the ''Deadweight'' in the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]]. According to Mandalore the Ultimate, the ''Courageous'' was melted down by the victorious Mandalorians, and Mandalore claimed that his battle [[Axe/Legends|axe]] was made from the [[Hull/Legends|hull]] of Karath's lost command.<ref name="KOTOR 20" /> Marn Hierogryph and his [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] companion, [[Slyssk]], barely escaped the bombardment when a panicking Slyssk seized command of a Republic ship that was slow to take off, inadvertently saving half a [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] that had been aboard. When they reached [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]], the two were approached by the [[Ministry of Defense]], which convinced the pair to become [[Propaganda/Legends|propaganda]] figures: Captain [[Benegryph Goodvalor]] and his trusty Trandoshan sidekick.<ref name="KOTOR 47" /> Not long afterward, [[Jervo Thalien]] of Lhosan Industries hired Hierogryph and Slyssk to track down Senator [[Haydel Goravvus]] on Taris, which was still undergoing occupation by Cassus Fett's forces; Hierogryph teamed up with the Hidden Beks [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] gang and helped them join forces with the Taris Resistance.<ref name="KOTOR 23" />

====The First Battle of Omonoth====
{{Quote|You've gone too far, Adasca! You don't have standing to offer a truce!''"<br />"''Don't I? I told you, gentlemen, this changes everything. Adascorp was a major corporate player. Now it is a ''galactic'' player. We no longer seek contracts. But we may accept ''allies''.|Admiral Saul Karath and Lord Arkoh Adasca|<ref name="KOTOR 19">[[Knights of the Old Republic 19|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 19]]</ref>}}
[[File:Omonothexogorths.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The weaponized exogorths that Adasca attempted to auction]]
Not long after the bombardment of Serroco, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Arkoh Adasca]] of [[the Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania]] forced the scientist [[Gorman Vandrayk]] to complete his work on the weaponization of the enormous space [[Slug (creature)/Legends|slugs]] known as [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorths]]. Believing that possession of the exogorths, which were essentially a galactic plague that could destroy entire planets, made him a galactic power, Adasca arranged for an auction of the exogorths aboard his ship, the ''[[Arkanian Legacy]]'', in the [[Omonoth system/Legends|Omonoth system]].<ref name="KOTOR 18">[[Knights of the Old Republic 18|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 18]]</ref> Adasca contacted representatives of the major players in the conflict—the Revanchist, Mandalore the Ultimate, and Admiral Karath—and requested their presence at his auction. The Revanchist was unable to attend in person, but after he and several Jedi foresaw that Adasca's offer could change the course of the war, he sent Alek in his place and instructed his friend to ensure that the danger they posed was eliminated.<ref name="KOTOR 19" /> The planet Telerath was caught up in mass panic upon the news that the ''Courageous'' was lost at Serroco, as Telerath was the next planet in the Mandalorians' path.<ref name="KOTOR 18" /> In the march to [[Omonoth]], the Mandalorians [[Battle of Nouane|ravaged]] the planet [[Nouane/Legends|Nouane]] and then [[Battle of Dagary Minor|took]] [[Dagary Minor]].<ref name="Atlas" /> Meetra Surik participated in the fighting at Dagary Minor, as did a soldier named [[Xaart]].<ref name="KOTOR II" />

By the time he reached the Omonoth system, Mandalore had used Rohlan Dyre's apparent death on Flashpoint as propaganda, using the Questioner as a martyr to advance the adoption of the Neo-Crusader cause. "Dyre" was the one who contacted Mandalore on Adasca's behalf, as he was accompanying Vandrayk's friend Jarael, whom Adasca was holding hostage to motivate Vandrayk, and upon meeting the warrior aboard the ''Arkanian Legacy'', Mandalore instructed the Questioner to remain "dead" for the cause.<ref name="KOTOR 20" /> Karath offered Adasca a seat on the Senate, but Mandalore countered with the offer of making Adascorp the sole weapons manufacturer of the Mandalorians. However, Adasca quickly became power-hungry and declared himself to be a new galactic power—but Adasca's auction came crashing down, thanks to the efforts of Onasi, Carrick, and Lucien Draay, who had been [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] by Adasca with Carrick upon his arrival at the ''Arkanian Legacy'' in order to keep Draay away from Adasca's plans. Donning the set of Neo-Crusader armor that Mandalore had given "Dyre" as a reward for his continued silence, Carrick staged a fight with Onasi, and then he and Draay revealed themselves as Jedi and declared that the entire affair had been a trap for Mandalore. Despite Adasca's protests, Mandalore believed their claims, and [[First Battle of Omonoth|chaos]] broke out aboard the ''Arkanian Legacy'' between the Mandalorians, Adasca's security forces, the Republic personnel, and Jedi.<ref name="KOTOR 21">[[Knights of the Old Republic 21|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 21]]</ref>

Amid the chaos, Carrick informed Vandrayk that Jarael was safe, prompting the inventor to seize control of the exogorths. Vandrayk used the exogorths to attack the ''Arkanian Legacy'', killing Adasca, before he took the exogorths with him to [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] in order to remove their installed [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] units and eliminate the threat they posed. All of Adasca's "guests" managed to escape the doomed ''Arkanian Legacy'', and while Carrick was called to Taris to meet with Hierogryph,<ref name="KOTOR 21" /> Karath returned to Coruscant. Karath promptly resigned his commission over the loss of Battle Group Serroco, but the Admiral of the Fleet refused to accept it and publicly commended Karath's performance; Karath was ultimately given command of the newly-built ''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship [[Swiftsure (Inexpugnable-class)|''Swiftsure'']].<ref name="Karath Home">{{KOTORbackups|22}}</ref> On Taris, the Taris Resistance [[Mission to the Jedi Tower|attempted to bomb]] the Jedi Tower, which Cassus Fett had been using as a command post, but Fett had already abandoned the Tower and attacked the Resistance in the [[Lower City]].<ref name="KOTOR 24">[[Knights of the Old Republic 24|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 24]]</ref>

====The advance is halted====
{{Quote|Alderaan? We're gonna knock over ''Alderaan''!''"<br />"''As soon as Cassus Fett brings the troop transports from Taris. The Republic won't expect a raid so far within their area of control. You scum like to steal? Now you can do it for a cause!|[[Wargo]] and a Rally Master|<ref name="KOTOR 26">[[Knights of the Old Republic 26|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 26]]</ref>}}
[[File:Jebblekotor27.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Mandalorian forces, gathered for an attack on Alderaan]]
Not long after the First Battle of Omonoth, as the Adasca affair came to be known, the Republic and Mandalorians both attempted to salvage the remains of the ''Arkanian Legacy'' and clashed in the [[Second Battle of Omonoth]]. The skirmish was inconclusive, and the concurrent [[Battle of Myrkr (Mandalorian Wars)|battle]] on Myrkr<ref name="Defense" />—the continuation of the Mandalorians' campaign from Wayland<ref name="Atlas" />—saw the Republic suffer approximately 2,000 casualties overnight. However, the Republic was able to secure a set of [[Neo-Crusader assault armor]] on Myrkr and began to study it for weaknesses.<ref name="Defense" /> From Myrkr, the Mandalorians moved on to [[Battle of the Thustra system (Mandalorian Wars)|conquer]] the nearby [[Thustra system/Legends|Thustra system]] and then [[Battle of the Obroa system|took]] the [[Obroa-skai system/Legends|Obroa-skai system]].<ref name="Atlas" /> However, their Coreward advance was brought to a halt after a series of events on Jebble, where the Mandalorians were preparing for their assault on Alderaan. [[Pulsipher]], Demagol's former assistant, uncovered the [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]] known as the [[Muur Talisman]] in the [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]] of Taris<ref name="KOTOR 25" /> and brought it to Jebble—but on the way there, the Talisman "bit" one of Pulsipher's subordinates. The [[Jedi Shadow]] [[Celeste Morne]], along with Carrick and Hierogryph, stowed away aboard Pulsipher's ship, and upon uncovering the Mandalorians' plans on Jebble, Carrick decided to warn the Republic.<ref name="KOTOR 26" />

Before Carrick could do so, the "bitten" Mandalorian transformed into a [[rakghoul]], a fearsome creature that spread the [[Rakghoul plague/Legends|rakghoul plague]]; the rakghoul quickly infected several other Mandalorians, and the plague began spreading like [[Fire/Legends|wildfire]].<ref name="KOTOR 26" /> Carrick managed to make contact with Cassus Fett, who had moved on from the Taris offensive, and warned the [[Field Marshal/Legends|Field Marshal]] of the events on Jebble. Pulsipher lost control of the Talisman, which held the spirit of its original owner, the Sith Lord [[Karness Muur]], when the Talisman attempted to take the Force-sensitive Carrick as its host, though Morne sacrificed herself in his place.<ref name="KOTOR 27">[[Knights of the Old Republic 27|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 27]]</ref> Under Muur's influence, Morne took control of the rakghouls—the Talisman was the original source of the plague—and quickly infected the entire Mandalorian army stationed on Jebble. However, Carrick eventually brought Morne to her senses, and Morne agreed to be locked inside an [[Dreypa's Oubliette|oubliette]], or stasis casket, to contain the Talisman's power. As Carrick and Hierogryph escaped from Jebble, Fett's forces arrived in the system and, to Carrick's horror, [[Disaster on Jebble|bombarded]] the planet with nuclear warheads as they did Serroco. The assault wiped out the rakghouls on Jebble,<ref name="KOTOR 28" /> and the loss of the Mandalorians' infected forces put an end to their planned Core World offensive.<ref name="Warfare" />

Around that time, the Republic arranged for Jedi Master [[Dorjander Kace]]—one of the Jedi Council members who was most strenuously opposed to the Revanchist's interventionist philosophy—to step down from the Council. Disillusioned with the Republic, Kace—who had fallen in love with a Mandalorian named [[Varda]] before her death in the Sith War—decided to join the Mandalorians, believing that he could change them for the better. Kace formed the [[Mandalorian Knights]], a group of like-minded Jedi who fought alongside the Mandalorians.<ref name="War 3">[[Knights of the Old Republic: War 3|''Knights of the Old Republic: War'' 3]]</ref> However, Kace was succeeded by the similarly anti-interventionist Lucien Draay, who issued arrest warrants for the Revanchist Jedi.<ref name="KOTOR 29">[[Knights of the Old Republic 29|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 29]]</ref> Shortly afterward, Coruscant was thrown into chaos by [[Vindication]], a pre-planned insurrection among the Jedi Order by the Jedi Covenant. Vindication was ordered by the Covenant's true leader, [[Haazen]], who had been manipulating the group for his own ends, and Haazen seized control of the Republic [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] around Coruscant, whose [[Tactical computer/Legends|tactical computers]] were linked by a tactic known as the [[Vanjervalis Chain]]. Haazen used the blockade's firepower to bombard Coruscant, eliminating the members of the Jedi Order who had been attempting to seize the [[Draay Estate]] just before Vindication;<ref name="KOTOR 32">[[Knights of the Old Republic 32|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 32]]</ref> the entire affair finally ended with Haazen's death and the destruction of the Draay Estate. The incident was covered up as a Mandalorian [[Terrorist/Legends|terror]] plot, and Carrick's and Hierogryph's names were cleared of all charges relating to the Padawan Massacre.<ref name="KOTOR 35">[[Knights of the Old Republic 35|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 35]]</ref>

====The Revanchists join the fight====
{{Quote|We began with nothing but a leader and a purpose—now we have an army. And ''sanction''! The High Council fell in line as soon as word spread—and the Republic is overjoyed! The Cathar ''didn't'' die in vain. Their fate, long ago, may have saved the galaxy!|Malak|<ref name="KOTOR 42" />}}
[[File:RevanIsBorn-KotOR42.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Revanchists on Cathar]]
Sometime after the Covenant affair, the Jedi Council arrived in force on Cathar to confront the Revanchists, who had ignored the Council's demand that they stand down.<ref name="KOTOR 42" /> Master [[Vandar Tokare]] had foreseen a great destruction that was to come to the galaxy in war, but the Council misinterpreted the vision—which actually referred to the forthcoming [[Jedi Civil War]]—as a warning against engaging Mandalore the Ultimate in battle.<ref name="Campaign" /> The Council warned the Revanchist and his followers that the Mandalorians were nothing special and ordered the movement to disband—but its tirade was interrupted when the Revanchist discovered a [[Revan's mask|Mandalorian mask]] underfoot. As the Revanchist picked up the mask, all of the Jedi present were suddenly enveloped in a shared Force vision of the past: They witnessed Cassus Fett herd the defenseless Cathar into the ocean and massacre them, despite the protests of the [[Gender/Legends|woman]] whose mask the Revanchist had found. Disgusted by the [[Speciecide|genocide]], the Revanchist donned the mask and, adopting the simpler name of Revan, vowed to wear it until all of the Mandalorians were brought to justice. In light of the vision, the Jedi Council begrudgingly sanctioned the Revanchists' intervention into the Mandalorian Wars,<ref name="KOTOR 42" /> though it still publicly denounced Revan's actions as unwise and too hasty.<ref name="KOTOR" />

Revan soon came up with a solution that appeased the Council and allowed the Revanchists to join the war: He revived the idea of a [[Republic Mercy Corps|Mercy Corps]] from the Sith War, in which Jedi were deputized to serve alongside the Republic Military allegedly as healers. The Council reluctantly agreed to Revan's request, and the Revanchists—now an official Mercy Corps—were placed under Revan's direct command. Revan and Alek, who by that time had adopted the name Malak, were appointed as [[General/Legends|generals]], and they worked with Captain [[Telettoh]] of the [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] ''[[Testament]]''.<ref name="KOTOR 42" /> In the following months, the Revanchists joined the Republic in battling the Mandalorians openly, and Revan proved himself a capable military leader by winning several victories against the Mandalorians.<ref name="KOTOR" /> As the year drew to a close, "Demagol" awoke from his coma and was put on [[Trial of Demagol|trial]] for his crimes, though Rohlan Dyre's claims as to his true identity were ignored. Fortunately for Dyre, he was rescued by Carrick and Hierogryph, who had realized that Demagol had switched places with Dyre.<ref name="KOTOR 47" /> In order to save his friend Jarael from Demagol, Carrick called in the favor that Cassus Fett owed him from Jebble: Carrick arranged for Fett's forces to encounter Admiral Karath near the [[Ottega system/Legends|Ithor system]] to draw out [[Dace Golliard]], a member of the Crucible slaving organization. Fett's forces staged an attack on Karath only to jump to hyperspace, and Golliard—who had been lurking nearby in order to capture the survivors of the battle—was captured by Karath, allowing Carrick to learn Jarael's location.<ref name="KOTOR 48" />

===The Mandalorian Triumph===
====Mandalorian Knights====
{{Quote|I know it offends some of you to have former Jedi in your ranks. Mandalorians win with arms, not tricks and magic! But that's why we joined your movement. My friends believe in a fair fight—not a corrupt Republic that leans on Jedi for protection! So this time, when some Jedi began fighting for the Republic, I didn't just mouth objections, like the Council. I set the balance right—''by giving the Mandalorians their own Jedi!''<s></s>|Dorjander Kace, addressing his Mandalorian soldiers on Halthor|<ref name="War 2">[[Knights of the Old Republic: War 2|''Knights of the Old Republic: War'' 2]]</ref>}}
[[File:Mount_Savage.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Dorjander Kace leads his Knights "against" the Mandalorians.]]
As the year [[3962 BBY]] began, Dorjander Kace developed a plan to give the Mandalorians their own Jedi by kidnapping [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] from the [[Jedi Enclave]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].<ref name="War 3" /> His plan was approved by Mandalore the Ultimate, who agreed to let Kace keep the younglings and train them on the planet [[Ordo/Legends|Ordo]], and Mandalore assigned a detachment of his warriors to assist Kace.<ref name="War 5">[[Knights of the Old Republic: War 5|''Knights of the Old Republic: War'' 5]]</ref> With Mandalore's support, Kace continued the campaign on the northern edge of the galaxy; once the Mandalorians [[Essien landing|captured]] the planet [[Essien]], he lured the Republic into an attack there. As the Republic lacked a regular army in that region, it was forced to rely on local [[Militia/Legends|militias]] to fight, and the [[Phaeda Militia|militia]] from the planet [[Phaeda/Legends|Phaeda]] was placed under the command of Captain Morvis and tasked with [[Essien landing|liberating]] Essien. The Republic was unaware of Kace's defection, and he and his Mandalorian Knights led Morvis's men—including Zayne Carrick, who had been drafted into the militia—in a charge against the [[Mandalorian bastion]] underneath [[Mount Savage]]. After "securing" the beach at the foot of the mountain, Kace ordered Morvis's forces to root out the Mandalorians in the forests, though Morvis chose to burn them down with [[Tibanna-six]] shells instead. Carrick attempted to warn the Mandalorians, as his pacifist nature would not let him stand by and allow the retreating Mandalorians to be massacred, but discovered that it was a trap—just as a swarm of Basilisk war droids descended on Morvis's command and captured them, with Kace's Mandalorian Knights at the lead.<ref name="War 1" />

Morvis's militia was drafted into the Neo-Crusaders despite the protests of his men, and Kace's Mandalorian Knights led their forces in a campaign northward toward Dantooine. The planet [[Halthor]] was one of the first targets, and the Mandalorian Knights led an [[Battle of Halthor|assault]] on a [[Halthor signal station|signal station]] manned by [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] [[Halthorian]]s.<ref name="War 2" /> Kace blackmailed Carrick into helping his plans—Carrick could either help Kace capture the [[Phaedacomm]] [[Relay station/Legends|relay station]] or watch the Mandalorians devastate his homeworld of Phaeda. Carrick developed a scam to get Mandalorians aboard the station by staging a fake viral outbreak aboard Morvis's captured ship, the [[Axehead frigate|frigate]] ''[[Reciprocity]]'', which was diverted to Phaedacomm, and the Mandalorians quickly captured the station. Upon discovering Kace's intended target,<ref name="War 3" /> Carrick recruited Morvis's crew to help him steal the Mandalorian [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]] ''[[Parjai]]'' as Kace's Mandalorian Knights took the ''Reciprocity'' to Dantooine. Carrick scammed Field Marshal [[Garon Borm]] and the ''Parjai''{{'s}} crew into fleeing the dreadnaught by repeating the viral outbreak scam, and he took the commandeered ''Parjai'' to Dantooine.<ref name="War 4" />

The Mandalorian Knights quickly took control of the Enclave and secured the students, and Carrick's attempt to con Kace into leaving ended in failure. Exposed, Carrick destroyed the ship on which Kace intended to extract the younglings, and Morvis's [[Troop/Legends|troops]] evacuated the younglings to the ''Reciprocity''. Carrick and Kace dueled in the Enclave when the latter tried to stop the escaping younglings, but when Kace learned that the Mandalorians had withdrawn support of his plans, Carrick managed to convince the former Jedi Master to surrender. Kace and his Knights were put on trial, where the former Jedi preached for nine hours straight about the righteousness of his cause; with the loss of the Mandalorian Knights, Mandalore the Ultimate abandoned the northern offensive and withdrew his forces from Halthor. In the aftermath of the campaign, Carrick secured a position as a lieutenant and special [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] agent attached to Morvis's ''Reciprocity''. In his new post, Carrick sought to serve as an "official conscience" for the Republic, teaching the military restraint in hopes of saving lives and bringing a peaceful end to the conflict.<ref name="War 5" />

====Coreward-bound====
{{Quote|You were at Serroco, when they turned the Stereb cities into glass craters. At Duro, when Basilisk war droids rained like meteors onto the orbiting cities, and when the Mandalorians set fire to the Xoxin plains on Eres III—the fires that burn still.|Atton Rand, to Meetra Surik|<ref name="KOTOR II" />}}
[[File:Thrantamandowar.png|thumb|left|250px|A naval battle during the conflict]]
Meanwhile, in the rest of the galaxy, the Mandalorian Wars entered a phase known as the [[Mandalorian Triumph]]—a campaign that first saw the Mandalorians [[Battle of Eres III|conquer]] the border world of [[Eres III]]<ref name="Atlas" /> and set fire to the planet's [[Xoxin plains|Xoxin]] plains, driving back Meetra Surik's forces from the world. The plains continued to burn over a decade afterward, earning the battle infamy.<ref name="KOTOR II" /> Also during this time, the Mandalorians continued their offensive from the Dernatine system, heading along the [[Lesser Lantillian Route]] to [[Battle of the Charros system (Mandalorian Wars)|conquer]] the [[Charros system/Legends|Charros system]], and then along the [[Great Kashyyyk Branch/Legends|Great Kashyyyk Branch]] and the [[Randon Run/Legends|Randon Run]] in an attempt to conquer the strategically-valuable [[Randon system/Legends|Randon system]]. However, the Mandalorians' [[Battle of the Randon system (Mandalorian Wars)|attempted conquest]] of Randon, which sat at the intersection of numerous routes, was defeated by the Republic. From Eres, the Mandalorians [[Battle of Azure|raided]] Azure and [[Battle of Contruum|subsequently]] the planet [[Contruum/Legends|Contruum]], and then headed south along the [[Vaathkree Trade Corridor/Legends|Vaathkree Trade Corridor]] to [[Battle of the Gizer system (Mandalorian Wars)|take]] the [[Gizer system/Legends|Gizer system]]. The [[Nazzri system/Legends|Nazzri system]] was [[Battle of the Nazzri system (Mandalorian Wars)|next to fall]], [[Battle of the Vena system|followed]] by the [[Vena system/Legends|Vena system]];<ref name="Atlas" /> after meeting up with additional forces at Dxun,<ref name="Warfare" /> the Mandalorians [[Battle of the Ambria system|took]] the [[Ambria system/Legends|Ambria system]] and [[Battle of the Zel system|then]] the [[Zel system]].<ref name="Atlas" />

With the advent of Jedi involvement in the war, the Republic unveiled the multitude of warships that they had been constructing in the shipyards of the Core and [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]], such as [[Centurion-class battlecruiser/Legends|''Centurion''-class battlecruisers]] and ''Hammerhead''-class [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]]. The [[Interdictor-class cruiser|''Interdictor''-class cruiser]] was also introduced at this time, a concept that had been pioneered by the late Admiral Veltraa.<ref name="Warfare" /> A number of manufacturing companies designed and marketed various new battle droid models in hopes of cashing in on the war effort, but the droids were not as successful as the companies had hoped.<ref name="Scavenger">''[[Scavenger's Guide to Droids]]''</ref> From the Zel system, the Mandalorians headed Coreward to [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]], where they [[Battle of Commenor (Mandalorian Wars)|smashed]] a Republic task force,<ref name="Warfare" /> and then [[Battle of the Quellor system (Mandalorian Wars)|conquered]] the [[Quellor system/Legends|Quellor system]].<ref name="Atlas" /> Fighting on the minor farming world of [[Churr]] near Quellor also saw heavy Republic losses.<ref name="Lessons">{{FFG|book=Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|text=''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook|story=Lessons from the Past}}</ref> After [[Battle of the Exodeen system (Mandalorian Wars)|taking]] the [[Exodeen system/Legends|Exodeen system]], the Mandalorians moved on to the shipyard world of [[Duro/Legends|Duro]],<ref name="Atlas" /> whose orbital cities and shipyards were bombarded by Basilisk war droids.<ref name="KOTOR II" /> Sometime around then, the Mandalorians also [[Battle of the Lantillies system|attacked]] the [[Lantillies system/Legends|Lantillies system]], though the Republic repelled their assault.<ref name="Atlas" />

Duro's infrastructure was devastated by the attackers, who destroyed many of the orbital space platforms in order to cut off the [[Corellian Trade Spine/Legends|Corellian Trade Spine]].<ref name="Campaign" /> However, Revan, Malak,<ref name="Warfare" /> and Meetra Surik<ref name="KOTOR II" /> managed to save the [[Battle of Duro (Mandalorian Wars)|Battle of Duro]] from becoming even more of a disaster. They arrived with a fleet of ''Interdictor''-class cruisers, thereby stopping the Mandalorians from escaping with war matériel stolen from Duro's shipyards. Bowing to public pressure, [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Tol Cressa]] appointed Revan [[Supreme Commander (Republic)|Supreme Commander]] of the Republic Military.<ref name="Warfare" /> Sometime during the course of the conflict, a fleet of Mandalorian warships [[Battle of Bespin (Mandalorian Wars)|attacked]] the [[Tibanna/Legends|Tibanna]]-mining planet of [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], which hosted a mining infrastructure built by the distant and [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]-rich planet [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]]. The attackers destroyed most of the mining operations in orbit of Bespin, hoping to break the Republic's supply of Tibanna gas.<ref name="Campaign" />

During the Mandalorians' Coreward push, the [[Koler system/Legends|Koler system]], a [[Red supergiant/Legends|red supergiant]] system on the edge of the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] near Empress Teta, saw fighting as well. The system had a reputation as a starship graveyard, which led the eccentric Jedi Master [[Val Isa]] to hide the [[Sanctuary (starship)|''Sanctuary'']]—a ''Hammerhead''-class cruiser converted into a mobile hospital and [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]—in the system as the fighting in the Core Worlds worsened. Isa had been providing rescue and medical services from the ''Sanctuary'' before taking refuge in the Koler system, making the ship a target, and Mandalorian forces [[Battle in the Koler system|attacked]] the ''Sanctuary'' after discovering it. The fighting claimed Isa's life and left the ''Sanctuary'' half-destroyed and locked in orbit around the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Koler]], though Isa held off the Mandalorian attackers long enough for her students to escape.<ref name="Lessons" />

===Republic counteroffensive===
====Driving them back====
{{Quote|It was not your ships or your men or your vaunted 'fight for freedom' that won this, the final battle of the war. It was by the actions of one person–the Jedi Revan–that you prevailed. His strategies and tactics defeated the best of us. Even Mandalore himself was taken aback by the ferocity of his attacks, the tenacity of his defenses, and the subtleties of his plans.|Canderous Ordo|audio=CanderousonRevan.flac|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
[[File:MandalorianWars-Tmeline8.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The Jedi lead the fight.]]
Over the course of [[3961 BBY]] and [[3960 BBY]],<ref name="Atlas" /> Revan directed the Republic Navy in taking the offensive against the Mandalorians. During that period, the Republic [[Liberation of Taris|liberated]] Taris, ending the lengthy siege and occupation of the world. Revan himself spearheaded the charge, leading a group of Jedi into battle in Taris's Undercity. While there, Revan freed a group of slaves who were about to be sold on the slave market, and one of them—a Force-sensitive Cathar named [[Juhani]]—was encouraged by one of Revan's compatriots to join the Jedi Order. Revan's tactical prowess saw the Republic achieve victory after victory, but it was not without a cost: Revan became increasingly willing to sacrifice populations and key planets in order to win victories elsewhere,<ref name="KOTOR" /> and Revan understood that the Republic's industrial might was the key to victory.<ref name="Warfare" />

"Moral shortcuts" became common under Revan's leadership as he and Malak began to adopt a policy of victory at all costs. Early in 3961 BBY, the pair discovered an [[Rakatan ruins (Dantooine)|ancient ruin]] of the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] species on Dantooine, where they found a [[Star Map]]—a relic of the Rakata that showed part of the location of the Rakatan [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as the [[Star Forge]].<ref name="KOTOR" /> Revan soon discovered another Star Map on the planet [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]],<ref name="Campaign" /> and not long afterward, he briefly visited the Sith tombworld of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Revan's visit to the planet of [[Malachor V]] saw the Jedi discover the [[Trayus Academy]], a temple of Sith learning, and Revan began to be seduced by the dark side of the Force as he learned from the Academy's knowledge. Though under the thrall of the dark side, Revan and Malak did not renounce the Jedi ways initially, and he and Malak continued to lead the Republic against the Mandalorians.<ref name="KOTOR" />

During this period, Mandalorian and Jedi forces clashed on the planet [[Vur IV]] for a period of several weeks, with the Mandalorians slowly losing ground. When the Mandalorians were ordered to abandon the planet and regroup in a neutral sector, a young recruit named [[Worr Muugyn]] refused the order and instead asked for volunteers to help him defend a fortified location they called [[Blood Ridge]]. Part of Muugyn's forces successfully held the ridge for several days, distracting more and more Jedi forces while the rest of Muugyn's group fortified other positions in the area and mounted a counterattack. The [[Battle of Blood Ridge]], as it came to be known, was later immortalized in Mandalorian culture, and [[Muugyn's Plan]] - the battle plan he sketched out on a piece of leather cut from his tabard - was considered a symbol of courage and determination to many clans.<ref name="Muugyn">{{TORcite|exp=lots|type=codex|Mandalorian Relics: Muugyn's Plan}}</ref>

From the Perlemian, Republic forces attacked planets in Mandalorian Space—the [[Vorzyd system/Legends|Vorzyd system]] was [[Battle of the Vorzyd system|first to fall]], and from there the Republic split their forces on two offensives. The [[Battle of the Lucazec system|attack]] on the [[Lucazec system/Legends|Lucazec system]] saw the Republic driven back, but the Republic was [[Battle of the Stenos system|successful]] in taking the [[Stenos system/Legends|Stenos system]] and [[Battle of the Elom system|then]] the [[Elom system/Legends|Elom system]]. From the Elom system, the Republic moved to [[Jaga's Cluster/Legends|Jaga's Cluster]].<ref name="Atlas" />

[[File:MandalorianWarsRepublicTrooper.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Republic ground troops during the war]]
However, the [[Battle of Jaga's Cluster]] ended in disaster—despite the presence of Revan and Malak, Cassus Fett's forces still managed to emerge victorious, and Fett gained notoriety for personally boarding one of the Republic flagships and killing the [[Unidentified Republic captain (Cassus Fett)|captain]] himself.<ref name="Jaga">{{CSWECite|II|133|Jaga's Cluster, Battle of}}</ref> Not long after, at the [[Battle of Althir (second)|Battle of Althir]], forces under Revan's command annihilated most of the Mandalorian ground forces, reclaiming the industrial world after around twenty-five years of Mandalorian control.<ref name="Atlas" /> Late in the Mandalorian Wars, Revan ordered his forces to [[Battle of Dxun|retake]] the planet Onderon and its moon Dxun, which was a heavily-fortified Mandalorian stronghold. A large-scale assault was organized to take Dxun, though the offensive was incredibly costly to the Republic—the enemy had decades to fortify the moon, with defenses that included [[Mine/Legends|minefields]], traps, anti-air [[Turret/Legends|turrets]], and the jungle beasts themselves.<ref name="KOTOR II" />

Revan's battle plan called for battle droids to be deployed on the moon to take out the anti-air turrets, and then his forces—under the command of the Jedi General Meetra Surik—undertook hundreds of small-unit feints to probe the Mandalorian defenses. Surik lost most of her forces when they charged across a minefield in an attempt to strike at the Mandalorian emplacements, but she carried out Revan's plans regardless. The battle lasted for months as the Mandalorians refused to give in, resulting in incredibly high casualties—veterans of the battle stated that the Republic lost ten soldiers for every Mandalorian killed. Despite the cost, the Republic ultimately captured the moon, driving the Mandalorians back toward the Outer Rim.<ref name="KOTOR II" />

====Showdown====
{{Main|Battle of Malachor V}}
{{Quote|I remember the ships. The last stand of the Republic. The tattered remnants of our fleet, the largest we could gather, but it was damaged, weakened and vulnerable. The Mandalorians couldn't resist. They tore into us like beasts, shredding our ships to scrap as we fought back. I remember the look you had when you turned to me. It was the longest you'd ever looked at me. You didn't say anything—just a nod. Events move quickly then, even in my dreams. Flashes, explosions, you—falling. I could feel the pain around me. And then the memory. The drifting hulks of the Mandalorian ships, the dead—allies, friends, strangers. And then the echo. Lingering. The sound I awaken to in my nightmares.|Bao-Dur|<ref name="KOTOR II" />}}
[[File:MtheUvsRevan.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Mandalore and Revan duel.]]
In 3960 BBY, Revan drove the Mandalorians increasingly farther back, and the Republic [[Second battle of the Lianna system|retook]] the Lianna system.<ref name="Atlas" /> Revan then forced a final confrontation at Malachor V, deploying a massive fleet to the planet in order to lure the Mandalorians into [[Battle of Malachor V|attacking]]. Revan also deployed a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] that he had prepared in secrecy: the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]], a [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] weapon designed by the Zabrak [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] [[Bao-Dur]]. The generator was intended as the centerpiece of the trap at Malachor, where Revan hoped to bring an end to the conflict. Revan split his forces in two, giving half to Meetra Surik to lure the Mandalorians into range of the superweapon and tasking her with the activation of the Mass Shadow Generator. However, Revan himself was delayed outside of the [[Malachor system/Legends|Malachor system]] by a Mandalorian scouting party, and by the time he arrived in the system, a massive [[Navy/Legends|naval]] battle was underway between Mandalore the Ultimate's fleet and Surik's ships.<ref name="Revan" />

Mandalore, realizing that defeat was inevitable due to the Republic reinforcements, challenged Revan to single combat aboard his [[Mandalore's flagship|flagship]]. Revan accepted his challenge and boarded the vessel, where he engaged in a duel to the death with the warrior chieftain. Despite Mandalore's strength, he was eventually overcome by his Jedi opponent; Revan struck down Mandalore, who ended up dying at the Jedi's [[Limb/Legends|feet]]. As the Taung coughed up [[Blood/Legends|blood]], he removed his mask and began to speak to Revan about how he had been betrayed. Mandalore explained how the Sith had manipulated him, as the Sith's spell had broken in Mandalore's last moments, and in order to prove his story, he gave the coordinates of the planet Rekkiad to Revan. As Mandalore died, Revan picked up Mandalore's Mask, the symbol of Mandalorian leadership, and took it as his spoils of war, preventing a new Mandalore from being chosen.<ref name="Revan" />

With Revan's victory over Mandalore, the Republic forces began to press the Mandalorian fleet ever closer to Malachor V, but they also suffered heavy casualties in the process, prompting Surik to command Bao-Dur to activate the Mass Shadow Generator. Both Revan and Surik's ships were out of range of the weapon, and they watched in horror as most of the Mandalorian fleet and a large part of the Republic fleet was drawn toward the planet into a massive gravity vortex. Tens of thousands died as hundreds of vessels collided with Malachor's surface, devastating the once-lush world and fracturing its core as the ships were crushed into the planet. The Republic suffered incredible casualties, though nowhere near as many as the Mandalorians, and the sheer number of deaths caused a substantial [[Wound in the Force/Legends|wound in the Force]]. Surik was the closest Jedi to the vortex to survive, and she instinctively severed her own connection to the Force to avoid being killed by the resulting shock wave. In the wake of the utter devastation, the remaining Mandalorians transmitted their unconditional surrender to the Republic.<ref name="KOTOR II" />

==Aftermath==
===The Republic===
{{Quote|When they left after the Mandalorian Wars ended, they were Jedi. When they returned… they were something else.|Carth Onasi, regarding Revan and Malak|audio=CarthRevanLeft.flac|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
[[File:DarthRevan_DarthMalak_EGF.png|thumb|left|250px|Darth Revan and Darth Malak, former heroes of the Mandalorian Wars]]
In the aftermath of the war, the Jedi Council demanded that the remaining Revanchists—namely, Revan, Malak, and Surik—return to Coruscant and face judgment for their actions during the end of the war. Revan and Malak refused and took the remainder of their forces into the Unknown Regions to the north, claiming to be pursuing the remaining Mandalorians. Surik chose to return to the Council, as she was bereft of the Force;<ref name="KOTOR" /> Malak urged Revan to use his new [[HK-47]] [[HK-series assassin droid|assassin droid]] to eliminate Surik, but Revan refused, claiming that she was "already dead."<ref name="KOTOR II" /> Revan had created HK-47 to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] selective opponents, as he sought to prevent the need for battles like Malachor.<ref name="KOTOR" /> Revan and Malak soon left their forces behind as they traveled to Rekkiad alone, seeking to confirm Mandalore's story. There, the two Jedi discovered Dramath's tomb and a [[Dramath the Second's datacron|datacron]] describing Dramath's history and the existence of a hidden Sith Empire; before departing Rekkiad, Revan chose to leave Mandalore's Mask in Dramath's tomb.<ref name="Revan" />

Revan and Malak followed the trail of the hidden Sith to the Empire's capital of [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], where they attempted to kill the Sith Emperor and prevent him from invading the Republic; however, the Emperor [[Qâzoi Kyantuska|dominated]] their minds and turned them fully to the dark side, making them his servants. Respectively anointing them Darth Revan and Darth Malak, [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords of the Sith]], the Emperor sent the two back as his advance agents to invade the Republic, tasking them with locating the Star Forge. Unbeknownst to the Emperor, the two broke free of the Emperor's control not long after departing Dromund Kaas, and they unknowingly adopted the Emperor's commands as their own desires.<ref name="Revan" /> Locating the Star Forge in the Unknown Regions, Revan and Malak used the space station to produce a massive fleet of warships, which they combined with their followers from the Mandalorian Wars, and they formed a [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|new Sith Empire]]. Admiral Karath was one of a number of high-ranking Republic [[Officer/Legends|officers]] who joined the new Sith, and in [[3959 BBY]], Revan led his Empire in invading the Republic. This subsequent conflict, which became known as the Jedi Civil War, lasted until [[3956 BBY]], when a redeemed Revan defeated his former apprentice at the [[Battle of Rakata Prime]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

A group of [[Refugee/Legends|refugees]], including a number of wealthy businessman, crash-landed their [[Transport/Legends|transport]] on the planet [[Makeb]] while fleeing Mandalorian raiders during the war. Makeb was located off major hyperspace routes, and over the next fifty years, the survivors—led by business magnate [[Semako Thalien]]—built settlements and re-established contact with the galaxy, using the planet's rich [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]] deposits and their own [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] and influence to transform Makeb into an immensely profitable world. Remaining largely independent from the Republic, Makeb attracted corporate leaders, banking enterprises, and wealthy tourists, and soon developed a number of luxury resorts.<ref name="Makeb">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=codex|History of Makeb}}</ref> The [[Interdiction field/Legends|Interdictor]] technology, the basis for the ''Interdictor''-class cruiser and the Mass Shadow Generator, was developed by Zabrak engineers during the conflict, and it became a frequently-used tactic in the [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] after the Mandalorian Wars.
